title: The Weight of Routine
content: For years, Olivia followed almost the exact same schedule every day without questioning it. She woke up at the same hour, took the same train, ordered the same coffee, and returned home exhausted every evening. At first, routines had made her life feel stable and organized. Over time, however, that predictability slowly transformed into something heavier. One evening, while standing inside a crowded train surrounded by tired faces, Olivia suddenly realized she could barely remember the last time she had done something unexpected. The thought unsettled her deeply. Although nothing was technically wrong with her life, she began wondering how many people quietly carried the same invisible exhaustion created by repetition and endless responsibilities.
